The Original Draft (Ungrounded but perfectly paced)
She wiped the chalk from her fingers onto her sleeve. White against blue.

Kel'Thuzad's head turned before hers did. Small motion. Attention lifting.

The wards answered a beat later.

The floor took the disturbance first. A pulse. Then another, farther out. The sanctum's old protections took the descent in order. No fumbling at the locks. No clumsy magical shove. Each threshold answered and handed the motion downward.

Kel'Thuzad stood. "Interesting," he said.

Jaina was already on her feet.

The next ripple came clearer. Stair. Landing. Stair again. Whoever was coming knew how to move through hostile ground and kept their pace through it.

Kel'Thuzad looked not at the door but at her. She understood why a moment later. The change had shown on her face before she could master it.

"Someone expected?" he asked.

Jaina did not answer.

Another pulse. Closer now. The wards answered one after another to a body driving through them without waste. A pause at one choke point. Choice made. Then movement again, cleaner than before.

Kel'Thuzad's eyes narrowed. "You know the approach."

She did. She knew that shape. Pressure crossing defended space without waste, every angle priced on the way through. She knew what kind of mind had entered.
